home.subtitle: AI agents pick libraries based on training-data bias, not actual quality. We fix that with real-time metrics from GitHub, npm, and package registries.
home.feature1Desc: Maintenance health, API clarity, doc quality, security posture, community signal, and deprecation safety.
home.feature2Desc: Rankings weighted for what matters to AI agents: clear APIs, good types, low dependency count, and active maintenance.
home.feature3Desc: Scores computed from live GitHub and registry metadata, not static popularity lists or training data.

categories.title: Categories
categories.subtitle: Browse open-source libraries by use case, ranked for AI agent usage.
categories.errorTitle: Unable to load categories. Is the API server running?
categories.errorHint: Run <command>make dev-api</command> to start the backend.

category.noLibraries: No libraries in this category yet.

library.aiExcellentDesc: Well-suited for AI agent usage. Clear APIs, good documentation, and active maintenance.
library.aiModerateDesc: Usable by AI agents but may have gaps in documentation or API clarity.
library.aiPoorDesc: May present challenges for AI agent integration. Consider alternatives.

recommend.title: Get Recommendations
recommend.subtitle: Describe what you need and get AI-agent-optimized library recommendations.
recommend.taskPlaceholder: e.g., make HTTP requests in TypeScript
recommend.noResults: No matching libraries found. Try a different description.

scoring.title: 6-Metric Scoring System
scoring.subtitle: Every library is scored on 6 metrics that matter most for AI agent usability. Scores are computed from live GitHub and npm data, not static popularity lists.
scoring.whyItMatters: Why it matters for AI agents
scoring.howP1: Each metric produces a score from 0 to 100. The overall score is a weighted average using the weights shown above.
scoring.howP2: Data is collected from the GitHub API and npm registry in real-time. Scores are refreshed during each collection run (daily when the worker is active, or manually via <command>make collect</command>).
scoring.howP3: Libraries flagged as deprecated (via npm or GitHub archived status) receive an overall score of 0, regardless of individual metric scores.
scoring.m1Desc: How actively is this library being maintained? Abandoned libraries are a risk for any project, but especially for AI agents that need reliable, up-to-date tools.
scoring.m1Why: AI agents often run unattended. A library that stops receiving updates can introduce silent vulnerabilities or incompatibilities that no one is around to fix.
scoring.m1f1: Commit frequency in the last 90 days
scoring.m2Desc: How easy is it for an AI agent to correctly use this library's API? Clear, consistent APIs reduce hallucination and improve code generation accuracy.
scoring.m2Why: AI agents generate code by pattern matching. Libraries with clear, typed APIs produce fewer errors and require less human review.
scoring.m3Desc: Is the documentation comprehensive enough for an AI agent to understand usage patterns? Good docs mean better generated code.
scoring.m3Why: AI agents learn usage patterns from documentation. Well-documented libraries with rich examples lead to higher quality code generation.
scoring.m4Desc: How secure is this library to depend on? Security matters for all projects, but especially when AI agents auto-install dependencies.
scoring.m4Why: When an AI agent recommends a library, it should not introduce security risks. Fewer dependencies and fast patching reduce exposure.
scoring.m5Desc: How strong is the community around this library? Community signals indicate long-term viability and ecosystem support.
scoring.m5Why: Libraries with strong communities get better support, more plugins, and are less likely to be abandoned. AI agents benefit from well-tested, battle-proven tools.
scoring.m5f3: Number of dependents (other packages using it)
scoring.m6Desc: Is this library at risk of deprecation? Using a deprecated library wastes time and creates migration burden.
scoring.m6Why: AI agents should never recommend deprecated libraries. This metric catches libraries that are winding down before they officially announce deprecation.
